reason: The text does not support the statement that Yahweh sees the people as evil with the exception of one righteous person. In this text fragment, Rahab, a Canaanite woman, is portrayed as righteous and faithful to Yahweh, even though she was not an Israelite. She showed kindness and faith in Yahweh, which led to the sparing of her and her family's lives when Jericho was conquered. This suggests that Yahweh does not view all the people as evil except for one righteous person, but rather recognizes righteousness and faith wherever it is found.
